title: mfa — PHP attribute contracts
description: One attribute. #[AsMfaListener] marks a class as a subscriber to MFA challenge + enrolment + credential lifecycle events (challenge issued, challenge succeeded, challenge failed, enrolment confirmed, credential registered, recovery-code consumed). Discovered at boot by the MfaServiceProvider so services under `notifications/`, `activity/`, `observability/`, and any tenant-defined listener can react without explicit event bindings.

attributes.name: AsMfaListener

params.name: events
params.description: Which MFA events this class reacts to. Wildcard `*` = every event under `Academorix\Mfa\Events\*`. Specific list e.g. `['MfaChallengeFailed', 'MfaRecoveryCodeConsumed']` subscribes to those only. Validated against the events map at boot; typos abort application boot.

params.name: queue
params.description: Queue name for asynchronous dispatch. Set to `null` for synchronous listeners (rare — reserve for observability tap-outs where the challenge outcome must have propagated before the response returns). Common values: `notifications` (default, mail/push channels), `notifications-critical` (P0 events like MfaChallengeSuspiciousActivityNotification or WebauthnCredentialDeleted), `audit` (write-heavy compliance streams).

params.name: priority
params.description: Listener execution order. Lower values run first. Use for cases where one listener's side-effect must precede another (e.g. `RevokeChallengeOnFailure` before `NotifySuspiciousActivity`). Framework default listeners occupy 1–99; user-defined listeners default to 100.

params.name: guards
params.description: Optional guard filter. When set, the listener only fires when the resolved Identity's caller principal matches one of the named guards (`sanctum` / `platform_admin`). Null = no guard filter. Useful when a listener should react to tenant challenges but not platform-admin re-verifications, or vice versa.
